title: Barcode Input Validation for Send-Out Manifest Specimens
description: Lab technicians add specimens to send-out manifests by typing barcodes into an input field and pressing an Add button. Currently there is no client-side validation — invalid barcodes are not caught until a server-side rejection, causing friction and re-entry errors. This story adds synchronous inline validation that catches format errors before submission, disables the Add button on invalid input, and resets the field after a successful add.
acceptance_criteria: When a barcode is entered that does not match the expected format (8–16 alphanumeric characters, no special characters), an inline validation error message reading 'Invalid barcode format' is displayed beneath the barcode input field and remains visible until the user corrects the input.
The Add Specimen button is disabled when the barcode input field is empty or contains a format-invalid value, preventing submission of barcodes that would fail server-side validation.

After a valid barcode is successfully added to the manifest specimen list, the barcode input field is cleared and any validation error state is reset, returning the form to a ready state for the next barcode entry.
examples: Entering 'ABC12345' (8 alphanumeric chars) is valid: no error shown, Add button enabled.
Entering 'AB12-CD56' (contains hyphen) is invalid: 'Invalid barcode format' error shown beneath input, Add button disabled.
Entering 'AB1' (only 3 chars) is invalid: error shown, Add button disabled.
Leaving the field empty: Add button is disabled but no error message is shown (field not yet touched).
After adding a valid barcode: input field clears, validation resets, technician can immediately enter the next barcode.
constraints: Validation must be client-side only — do not modify any HTTP service calls, API contracts, or server-side behavior.
Validation error display must follow the existing PrimeNG form error pattern used elsewhere in the sendouts or shared UI library.
Do not add a new button — wire the disabled state to the existing Add Specimen button.
Validation state must not persist into the NgRx Signals store or affect saved manifest data.
Scope is limited to the barcode entry sub-form within the send-out manifest specimens section.
non_functional_requirements: Validation must run synchronously on each keystroke (no async validators) to avoid UI flicker or delayed feedback.
The inline error message element must carry an appropriate data-test-id attribute and be exposed through the Cypress test harness so it can be asserted in component tests.
The barcode input and Add button must each have data-test-id attributes if they do not already — add them as part of this story.

raw_input_notes.purpose: Medium-complexity agent-runner test story — exercises multi-task decomposition, codebase discovery, and Angular signal patterns
raw_input_notes.domain: Mayo Collaborative Services — RLS Send-Outs feature (libs/pearls/sendouts/)
raw_input_notes.difficulty_rationale: Requires the task-generator to identify 2-3 subtasks with a clear dependency boundary (validation logic before UI wiring), requires the software-engineer-hyperagent to locate the correct component and store in an unfamiliar codebase, and requires the QA agent to verify both the happy path and the invalid-input path. Scope is bounded and reversible.
